page: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by dual Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4 GB of RAM per physical core. This bare-metal instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights the server's multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark CPU encryption tests all ranking in the top tier. Memory performance is similarly robust, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 337.73 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% of tested configurations. Single-core performance, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, falls into the average tier, indicating that the platform's strength lies in highly parallelized workloads rather than single-threaded execution speed.

Given its high core density, 512 GB memory capacity, and 50 Gbps network throughput, the m6i.metal is suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale language model prompt processing. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the return on hardware resources for enterprise-grade multi-threaded applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 512 GB of DDR4 memory and 50 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high-speed large-block memory read bandwidth of 337.73 GB/sec, and strong database operation capabilities. Conversely, single-core benchmarks remain in the average tier, indicating that the system is optimized for parallel processing. The server is highly suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database hosting, software compilation, and static web serving where bare-metal access and high resource density are required without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density, bare-metal platform delivering 128 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB RAM for demanding multi-threaded enterprise applications.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.5 GHz
512 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier large-block read bandwidth
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity
Optimized for multi-threaded databases, software compilation, and web serving
